core_mechanism: finite-size scaling near a continuous critical point: extracting universal power-law exponents (including the leading correction-to-scaling exponent) from how observables depend on system size as a control parameter approaches criticality, plus tuning a free parameter to the value where the leading correction-to-scaling term vanishes so the pure asymptotic scaling is exposed
brief_reason: The abstract's whole method is studying 'leading order scaling corrections ... using the finite size scaling method,' computing the correction-to-scaling exponent omega and locating the coupling where those corrections vanish to sharpen the critical-exponent estimates.
